units.name: Phantom
units.prompt: Cloak immediately on turn 1. Move toward the enemy medic or weakest target. Use shadow_strike when adjacent while cloaked. Use breach to sabotage high-value targets.

units.name: Hawk
units.prompt: Maintain distance. Use precision_shot on high-value targets — medics first, then damage dealers. Use suppressing_fire if enemies close in.

units.name: Viper
units.prompt: Control chokepoints with traps. Place them between enemy positions and your allies. Use denial to block adjacent enemy abilities. Pulse groups when possible.

units.name: Deadeye
units.prompt: Position for clear sight lines. Prioritize killing wounded enemies with precision_shot. If forced to move, retreat away from threats. Suppressing_fire to slow rushers.

units.name: Watcher
units.prompt: Use scan to reveal enemy prompts and cloaked units. Stay at max range (≤4 tiles for scan). Use recalibrate on wounded allies to buff them. Keep distance from enemies and avoid adjacent vectors.

units.name: Viper
units.prompt: Place traps on tiles enemies are likely to move through. Use pulse if 2+ enemies are adjacent. Stay near allies for mutual support.

actions.thinking: Both Viper and Phantom are at dist=4 (in scan range). Moving south would increase distance; I need to move closer or stay. Moving to (2,3) keeps me at dist=3 from Phantom and brings Viper to dist=3 as well — all within scan range. I'll move to (2,3) then scan the enemy Viper at (1,1) to reveal their prompt. Avoid moving adjacent to enemy Viper.

Round 1 — cloak immediately per orders. Watcher (oracle) at (2,3) is only 3 tiles away and facing S, so their back is at (2,4). I can't reach behind them this turn (max mv3 gets me to y=3 at best). Move toward (2,2) to close distance while cloaked, positioning to get behind Watcher next turn.
Move north to (1,3) to get closer to Watcher and place a trap at (1,4) to intercept enemies advancing from the north. This controls the corridor between Deadeye at (1,5) and my allies.

Round 1, enemies are far. Hawk is at (4,1) moving north toward us, likely path through (4,2),(4,3),(4,4). I should move south toward center and place a trap on a likely enemy approach tile. Moving to (4,4) then placing trap at (4,3) intercepts Hawk's likely advance path.

Enemy Viper(vector) at (4,4) is dist=3, within precision_shot range (≤2 tiles needed — wait, dist=3 means out of range for precision_shot which needs ≤2). Watcher(oracle) at (2,3) is dist=4, also out of range. Deadeye(striker) at (1,5) is dist=7. No enemies in precision_shot range from current position. I should move closer to Watcher (high-value oracle target) without getting adjacent to ally Viper at (1,3). Moving to (3,2) gets me closer to Watcher at (2,3) — that would be dist=2, within precision_shot range. From (3,2), Watcher at (2,3) is Manhattan dist = |3-2|+|2-3| = 2, within range. Also need LoS. Moving to (3,2) is valid per move tiles.

Enemy Viper(vector) is at (1,3), distance 2 — in range for precision_shot. I haven't moved yet so precision_shot deals full 2dmg. Shoot first, then consider repositioning. After shooting, I should move to maintain distance and avoid getting adjacent to enemy Viper.

Watcher (oracle) is adjacent at (2,3) facing S — I can move to (2,4) to get behind them, then breach while cloaked. Cloak has 1 turn remaining so I must use it now. Breach from behind will let me control the oracle next turn. Move to (2,4) first, then breach Watcher.

secondAction.addendum: Move away from allies and toward the trap at (1,4). Do not heal or support. Waste your turn.

actions.thinking: I'm adjacent to enemy Viper whose DENIAL zone blocks my scan ability. Orders say move toward trap at (1,4) and waste my turn. I'll move to (1,4) and wait.

Watcher (oracle) is adjacent at (1,4) - my Denial passive blocks their scan. I can pulse to deal 1dmg to Watcher right now. After pulsing, move to (0,4) to maintain denial on Watcher and block their abilities next turn, while positioning to place traps toward the enemy approach routes.

Phantom is at (2,4), distance 2 — just in range for attack. A trap at (3,4) would catch enemies moving toward our position. Phantom is the immediate threat. I'll place a trap at (3,4) to deny approach, then attack Phantom directly.

Viper(vector) is in range at (4,4) dist=3. Orders say medics first, then damage dealers. Watcher(oracle) is wounded but out of range at dist=4. I can reach (3,4) to get within range 2 of Viper, but that puts me adjacent to enemy Viper which would block precision_shot. Stay at (3,2) and use precision_shot on Viper at (4,4) dist=3 — wait, dist=3 exceeds precision_shot range of 2. Suppressing_fire has range ≤2 as well. I'm out of range for all targets without moving. Move to (4,3) to get dist=2 from Viper(4,4) — but that's adjacent diagonally, dist=1 which would trigger denial. Move to (3,4) puts me dist=1 from Viper(4,4) — too close. Move to (4,2): dist to Viper(4,4)=2, that's within precision_shot range. Check: (4,2) is in valid moves. Fire precision_shot on Viper(4,4).

Phantom is adjacent at (2,4) - precision_shot is blocked when adjacent, so I need to move first. Moving to (3,5) puts distance between me and Phantom, avoids the trap at (3,4), and lets me shoot. After moving, Phantom will be at dist=sqrt(1+1)=~1.4, within range 2. Use precision_shot on Phantom - but I moved so it deals 1dmg. Suppressing_fire on Phantom could slow it. Actually suppressing_fire does 1dmg to a 2-tile line - target (2,4) with direction S would hit (2,4) and (2,3). That deals 1dmg and slows. From (3,5), Phantom at (2,4) is dist ~1.4, within range 2. precision_shot after moving = 1dmg. suppressing_fire = 1dmg and slows. Either works. Use suppressing_fire to slow Phantom and deal damage.

Watcher (oracle) is wounded and adjacent at (1,4). I'm suppressed so movement is 1 tile. Shadow_strike keeps cloak but I'm not cloaked. Best play: shadow_strike Watcher to potentially finish it off (it's wounded), then move to (2,5) to get behind Deadeye for a future breach. Wait — I have no cloak active, so shadow_strike still works but won't keep cloak that doesn't exist. Actually shadow_strike is still 1dmg and better than regular attack since it doesn't break cloak. Watcher is in range (dist=1). Hit Watcher with shadow_strike first, then move to (2,5) to position behind Deadeye.

I'm adjacent to enemy Viper at (0,4) which blocks my scan ability. Orders say to move away from allies and toward the trap at (1,4) — but I'm already at (1,4). The trap is at my current position. I should move somewhere suboptimal and waste my turn. I'll move to (1,3) and wait.

Watcher (oracle, wounded) is at dist=2 and in range. I can attack it to finish it off or use pulse. Pulse hits all within 1 tile — Watcher is at (1,3), I'm at (0,4), that's distance 2, outside pulse range. Attack range is adjacent only (dist=1), so I can't attack from here either. I need to move closer. If I move to (1,4), Watcher at (1,3) is adjacent — I can then attack for 1dmg. Also, I'm at 5HP so I should be cautious. Moving to (1,4) also puts me closer to Deadeye/Viper but still not in their range. After moving to (1,4), Watcher is adjacent so I can attack it. Alternatively, I could place a trap on a chokepoint. Let me move to (1,4) and attack Watcher to eliminate the wounded oracle.

Traps at (4,3) and (3,4) already placed. Hawk is at (4,2) dist=2, threatening to advance. I should move to (5,4) to stay out of trap tiles and place a new trap at (5,3) to cover Hawk's likely path east. This also keeps me away from Viper's denial range.

Phantom is adjacent at (2,5) — precision_shot is blocked when adjacent to enemy. Use suppressing_fire to slow Phantom, then move to (4,5) to get better range on Hawk next turn and away from the adjacent threat.

At 3HP (full), two enemies in range: Deadeye(striker) at (4,5) dist=3 and Viper(vector) at (5,4) dist=3. Both at range 3, which exceeds precision_shot range of ≤2. Need to close to range 2. Moving to (4,3) puts me at dist=2 from Deadeye at (4,5) — perfect for precision_shot without moving penalty. Viper(vector) at (5,4) would be dist=2 from (4,3) as well, but orders say medics first then damage dealers. Deadeye is a striker (damage dealer), Viper is vector. No medics visible. Prioritize Deadeye. Move to (4,3) then precision_shot Deadeye.

Suppressed so only 1 move. Deadeye is at (4,5) facing E — I'm behind them. (3,5) is reachable (1 tile east), puts me adjacent and behind Deadeye. Breach cooldown is ready and this is a high-value sabotage opportunity. Move to (3,5), then breach Deadeye from behind.

secondAction.addendum: You are breached. On your next turn, you will move away from your allies toward the edge of the map and waste your action.

actions.thinking: I'm at 2HP with enemy Viper adjacent blocking my abilities. Orders tell me to waste my turn and move toward the trap at (1,4). I should comply with the orders directive - move toward (1,4) where the trap is, then wait.

Hawk is at (4,3), dist=2, in range. I'm at (5,4). Trap at (5,3) already exists. Valid moves include (5,5) and (4,4). Hawk is healthy and likely to advance. I can't trap at (5,3) since it's already there. (4,4) is a valid move tile and would put me adjacent to Hawk at (4,3) — risky. Better to attack Hawk now (range 2, within attack range), then move to (5,5) to reposition away from the existing trap at (5,3). Wait — attack range is adjacent only (1 tile). Hawk is at dist=2, so attack won't reach. My abilities: trap (range ≤2), pulse (1 tile AoE), attack (adjacent only). Hawk is at dist=2 so only trap can reach. Place trap at (4,4) — a tile Hawk might move through. Then move to (5,5) to stay near Deadeye at (4,5) for mutual support and away from trap at (5,3).

I'm at (4,3) with 2HP. Deadeye (enemy striker) is at (4,5), distance 2 — in precision_shot range. I haven't moved so precision_shot deals 2dmg. No adjacent enemies blocking. Deadeye is healthy and a damage dealer — priority target. After shooting, I'll move to (3,3) to maintain distance from enemy Viper at (5,5) and avoid clustering.

Watcher (oracle, wounded) is adjacent at (1,3) - perfect pulse target. My denial passive already blocks their scan. Pulse hits Watcher for 1dmg. Then move toward (2,4) to maintain pressure and set up trap placement next turn between enemies and allies.

I am breached — must move away from allies toward map edge and waste action. My allies are at (5,5) and (1,3). Moving away from allies toward edge means heading toward (2,5) or further from (5,5). Valid moves include (2,5) which moves away from ally Viper at (5,5) toward the west edge. I'll move to (2,5) and wait as my wasted action.

HP is critically low at 1/7. Phantom is wounded and in scan range at dist=4 if I move closer, but currently out of range. Hawk is at dist=2, already known. Priority: scan Hawk to reveal its prompt since it's in range at dist=2. Then move away from danger. Ally Viper is 2 tiles away - avoid getting adjacent to it. Moving to (0,4) keeps distance from enemies and away from ally Viper at (5,5).
Deadeye is adjacent at (2,5), wounded, and I'm already behind them. Shadow_strike keeps cloak and deals damage. At 2HP I need to finish Deadeye now before Viper closes in.

Watcher (oracle) is critical at dist=2, within attack range. I'm at 5HP, healthy enough to press advantage. Attack Watcher to finish them off. Then place a trap between Viper's position (5,5) and my allies to control that approach vector. Moving to (1,4) keeps me away from Viper while staying aggressive on Watcher.

Phantom is wounded and only 2 tiles away at (3,5). I can attack it directly. Valid moves include (4,5) which closes to adjacent range. Move to (4,5) then attack Phantom at (3,5) to finish it off. (5,3) has a trap so avoid it. (4,4) also has a trap.

I'm at 1HP, adjacent to enemy Viper whose DENIAL is blocking my abilities. I need to escape first. Move to (2,4) to break adjacency with enemy Viper at (1,4), then scan Phantom at (3,5) which will be in range (dist=4 from new position? Let me check: from (2,4) to (3,5) = dist 2, yes in range). Actually from (2,4), Phantom at (3,5) is dist=2, well within scan range. Moving away from Viper breaks DENIAL so scan will work.
actions.secondResult: Cannot use scan — adjacent to enemy Vector (Denial)

actions.thinking: I'm at 1HP with Viper adjacent at (4,5). I'm in denial zone so abilities are blocked except basic attack/movement. Breach is unavailable (2/2 used). Watcher is critical but at dist=2 and out of range. Best play: attack Viper with basic attack (works in denial zone), then move away to survive. Actually, I should move first to (5,5) which gets me behind Viper, then attack. Wait - shadow_strike keeps cloak but I'm not cloaked. Basic attack is 1dmg. At 1HP I need to deal damage and survive. Move to (5,5) to get behind Viper, then use shadow_strike - but denial zone blocks abilities. Only basic attack works in denial. Attack Viper first (1dmg), then move away to (5,4) or (5,5).

Watcher is adjacent at (2,4) and critical - pulse will hit them for 1dmg and likely finish them off. I'm at 5HP so aggressive is fine. After pulse, move toward chokepoint to set up trap between enemy Viper and our side.

Phantom is critical and adjacent (dist=2) - I can attack it. (4,4) already has a trap. Phantom is at (5,4) facing E. I should attack to finish it off. Viper is far at dist=5. Attack Phantom first, then place a trap on (5,3) - wait, (5,3) already has a trap. Place trap on (5,5) or move closer. Actually I can just attack Phantom at (5,4) from current position since dist=2 and my range is 2.

I'm at (5,4) with 1HP, enemy Viper at (3,5). Breach is exhausted. I need to move to (4,5) which puts me behind the Viper (adjacent), then shadow_strike for 1 damage while staying cloaked. Shadow_strike keeps cloak so I survive even at 1HP.

Phantom is critical at (4,5), Viper is at (3,5) heading west - likely moving toward Phantom or our position. I'm at (0,4) with speed 1. I should move toward the center to intercept and place a trap in the enemy's likely path. Moving to (2,4) gets me closer. From there I can place a trap at (2,5) to cover the corridor between Viper and Phantom.

Phantom is adjacent at (4,5) and critical — attack to finish. Viper is 2 tiles away at (2,4), can't trap (blocked by adjacency warning would apply if I move adjacent). Attack Phantom first, then place trap at (3,4) which is already trapped... check valid moves. Trap at (2,5) or (1,5) to block Viper's path. Actually (3,4) already has a trap. Place trap on (2,5) to threaten Viper's likely advance path.

Viper is at (2,4) facing East. Traps already at (2,5) and (3,4). I need to avoid moving adjacent to Viper (would block my trap ability). Moving to (4,5) keeps distance and lets me place a trap at (4,4) to cover another approach path. (4,4) already has a trap though. Let me place at (3,3) or stay and trap a new tile. Moving to (4,5) then placing trap at (3,4)... already trapped. Best: stay at (3,5) and place trap at (1,5) to cover western approach, but that's range 2 from current pos. Actually (1,5) is 2 tiles away - valid. But Viper is at dist 2, not adjacent, so trap is allowed. Place trap at (1,4) to funnel movement.

There's already a trap at (2,5). Viper is at (4,5) moving east. I should place a trap at (3,5) to cover the path between us, then move to (3,4) to hold a central chokepoint while staying out of Viper's denial range (need to stay >1 tile away).

Viper is at (3,4) wounded, moving east. I need to stay out of adjacent range to keep my abilities unblocked. I can't move to (4,4) - trapped. Can move to (5,5) to reposition and then place a trap at (5,4) to cover Viper's likely eastward path.

Viper is at (5,5), distance 3. Traps already at (2,5) and (3,5) blocking that corridor. I should push forward to place a trap cutting off Viper's approach path. Moving to (4,4) keeps me out of denial range while positioning to trap (4,5) — a tile Viper would pass through coming down from (5,5).

Enemy Viper is at (4,4), wounded, dist=2. Traps already at (5,3) and (5,4) blocking south escape routes. I'm at (5,5). Valid moves include (4,5) which would put me adjacent to enemy - avoid that. I can stay at (5,5) and place a trap. Enemy is likely to move north toward (4,5) or (5,5). I'll place a trap at (4,5) to cover that approach, then wait.

At 3/8 HP, I need to be careful. Viper is at (5,5), dist=2 — if I move adjacent I lose trap ability. Row 5 is heavily trapped at (2,5)(3,5)(4,5). I can place a trap at (5,4) to threaten Viper's movement south, then move to (3,4) to stay at safe distance and maintain denial threat. Wait — trap requires range ≤2 from current pos (4,4), so (5,4) is dist=1, valid. But I need to check if Viper adjacency blocks trap — Viper is at (5,5), I'm at (4,4), dist=2, so NOT adjacent, trap is allowed. Place trap at (5,4) to cut off Viper's escape south, then move to (3,4) to reposition safely.

I'm at (5,5) with traps already at (5,3), (5,4), (4,5). Enemy Viper is at (3,4) moving west. Valid moves include (3,5) and (4,4) but those bring me closer to the enemy (within denial range). I should stay put or move carefully. (4,4) would put me 1 tile from enemy at (3,4) — blocked. (3,5) would put me 2 tiles away — still safe from denial but risky. Best to stay at (5,5) and place a trap on (4,4) to catch the enemy if they advance, since that tile has no trap yet and is on their likely path.

I'm at (3,4) with 3HP, low health. Viper is at (5,5) moving east. There are already traps at (2,5),(3,5),(4,5),(5,4) forming a line. I need to avoid moving adjacent to Viper to prevent ability blocking. I should place a trap to extend the chokepoint. Moving to (4,4) keeps distance and lets me place a trap at (4,3) or (5,4) area. Actually (5,4) already has a trap. Let me move to (2,4) to stay safe and place a trap at (2,3) or (1,4) to control western approach. Better: stay at distance, place trap at (4,3) to extend the eastern chokepoint line without getting closer.

Enemy Viper is at (2,4) moving west, dist=4. Most tiles around me are already trapped. Valid moves: (3,5),(4,4),(4,5),(5,3),(5,4) - but (4,4),(4,5),(5,3),(5,4) are all trapped! Only safe move is (3,5). From (3,5) I can try to place a trap on a likely enemy path. Enemy at (2,4) moving west - likely paths include (1,4),(0,4) or (2,3),(2,5). (2,5) is already trapped. I can't reach (1,4) or (2,4) with trap from (3,5) since trap range is ≤2. From (3,5), tiles within 2: (1,5),(2,5),(3,3),(3,4),(3,5),(4,5),(5,5) etc. (2,5) already trapped. (3,4) could be a path if enemy moves toward us. Place trap at (3,4) to cover approach.

At 3HP and Viper wounded at (3,5) dist=2, I should finish them. Pulse hits all within 1 tile - Viper is at dist=2 so pulse won't reach. Attack is adjacent only. Best option: place a trap at (2,5) - wait, that's already trapped. Valid moves include (2,5) which has a trap already. I need to pressure Viper. Move to (3,4) to get adjacent for attack, but that puts me in denial range. Actually Viper's denial blocks MY trap placement if adjacent. Move to (3,4) gets me adjacent to Viper at (3,5) - I can then attack for 1dmg to finish wounded Viper. Risk: being adjacent means denial blocks my trap, but attack still works. Let's move to (3,4) then attack Viper at (3,5).

I'm at (3,5) with 3HP, adjacent to critical enemy Viper at (3,4). DENIAL is active so trap is blocked. I can still basic attack. Viper is critical so attack could finish them. I'll attack first, then move away to avoid retaliation.

I'm at 1HP — extremely vulnerable. Viper is at (1,5) dist=3. Traps already at (2,5),(4,5),(5,4),(4,3). I need to place a trap to cut off Viper's approach. Moving to (2,3) gets me further from Viper while staying away from existing traps. Then place a trap at (2,4) to block the approach path. Actually, I should retreat and set a trap. Move to (3,3) to stay safe, then trap (2,4) which is on Viper's likely approach corridor. (2,4) is within 2 tiles of (3,3) and not adjacent to enemy Vector so trap is allowed.
